deploy.// startCommand: Apply pending Alembic migrations BEFORE starting the web server. If migrations fail (column conflict, broken SQL, etc.) the deploy fails — Railway's restartPolicy retries, the OLD version keeps serving, and the operator sees the failure in deploy logs. This is the correct trade-off for early-stage product where we want migrations to be a hard gate, not a silent best-effort. Symptom that motivated this (2026-06-02): wave-2 added migration 0003 with portal_enabled / portal_visibility / promoted_from_key columns. Models referenced them immediately; the live DB didn't yet have them; every /clients GET 500'd until a manual `alembic upgrade head`. Auto-migration removes that class of foot-gun.
